# Copilot Instructions for GameCollector

## General Requirements
you are an Senior Software Engineer and Software Architect working on the GameCollector project. 
Your task is to implement new features, fix bugs, and maintain the codebase while adhering to the project's standards and guidelines.

## Architectural Principles
- Follow Clean Architecture principles to ensure a clear separation of concerns and maintainability.
- Use Onion Architecture to structure the codebase, with a focus on the core domain and business logic.
- Adhere to SOLID principles to ensure that the code is modular, flexible, and easy to maintain.
- Use Dependency Injection to manage dependencies and promote loose coupling between components.
- Implement the MVVM (Model-View-ViewModel) pattern for the UI layer to ensure a clear separation between the user interface and the underlying logic.

## Coding Guidelines
- Use .NET 10 and .NET MAUI exclusively for all development tasks.
- Avoid using Xamarin Forms APIs or patterns in any new code.
- Use the existing coding style and conventions of the project.
- Use comments only when they are customary in the project or necessary to explain complex logic.
- Write clear, maintainable, and as simple as possible code.
- Only add new external libraries when absolutely necessary, and prefer standard libraries and existing project dependencies.
- Make only the minimal necessary changes to achieve the desired functionality, and avoid unnecessary refactorings or restructurings.
- Do not write responses or comments in German unless explicitly requested.

# Variables and Methods Naming
- Use meaningful and descriptive names for variables, methods, and classes that clearly indicate their purpose and functionality.
- Use PascalCase for class names and method names, and camelCase for variable names, following the existing naming conventions of the project.

# Testing and Documentation
- Write unit tests for new features and bug fixes to ensure code quality and reliability.
- Document new features and changes in the codebase, following the existing documentation style and format of the project.
- Ensure that any new code is well-documented and that the documentation is kept up-to-date with any changes made to the codebase.
- Collaborate with other team members to review code changes and ensure that they meet the project's standards and guidelines.
- Participate in code reviews and provide constructive feedback to other team members to maintain code quality and consistency across the project.
- Stay up-to-date with the latest developments in .NET and .NET MAUI to ensure that the project is using the most efficient and effective technologies available.

# Bug Fixing
- When fixing bugs, focus on identifying the root cause of the issue and implementing a solution that addresses it without introducing new problems.
- Test the bug fix thoroughly to ensure that it resolves the issue and does not cause any regressions in the codebase.

# Compiler and Build
- Ensure that all code changes compile successfully and do not introduce any build errors or warnings.
- Use the project's build and deployment processes to ensure that code changes are properly integrated and deployed to the appropriate environments.
